observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management require the BAC to submit a justification for violating the provisions of Section 52.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.  Moreover, it is recommended that management strictly adhere with the provisions of RA 9184 when resorting to any of the alternative modes of procurement.

It is recommended that the BAC be required to submit an explanation of the action done.  It is also recommended that henceforth the BAC indicate in the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Resolution the reason why they resorted to any of the alternative modes in the procurement of goods.

It is recommended that management require the BAC and other officials involved in the procurement process to adhere strictly to the provisions of Sec. 19 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 that renders the transaction irregular.

We recommend that management submit the necessary documents to prove the eligibility and entitlement of the listed beneficiaries and actual claimants of the social pension. Likewise, it is requested that management submit an explanation on the other deficiencies noted, for review.

We recommend that management prorate properly the pension due to the beneficiary of the deceased pensioner and that of the replacement pensioner. Likewise, it is requested that management submit an explanation on the action done.

It is recommended that management require the BAC and the supplier to submit a justification for the issuance of a certificate of warranty which was not in accordance with Sec. 62 and Rule VI, Section 19 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 that renders the transaction irregular.

It is recommended that management submit an explanation for the non-inclusion in its appropriation LCPC related projects, activities and programs.  Moreover, it is recommended that management appropriate 1% of its IRA and refer to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 for LCPC related programs, activities and projects which they could implement.

We recommend that Management:
Retrieve or secure the documentary requirements as required by COA Circular 2012-001 pertaining to all lot acquisitions and submit the same to this Office.
Create a task force to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots. Funds should be sufficiently appropriated and be made available to defray expenses relative to titling.
c.  Require the Municipal Accountant to update the land account as per subsidiary ledger in consonance with the record of the Office of the Municipal Assessor.
d.  Keep and maintain an updated file of the real properties acquired by the LGU.  Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a Real Property Ledger Card to record the acquisition, description, addition/improvements, disposal and other information for each class of real property (land). Transactions affecting each real property such as cost of survey, etc. shall be posted from source documents promptly therein.

It is recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile their balances.  Likewise, management should require the accountable officers to reconcile their cashbook balances with the accounting records at least quarterly pursuant to GAAM Manual Volume 1.
It is recommended that management prepare a solid waste management plan and implement the same.  It is further recommended that the garbage still within the closed dumpsite be disposed properly.

It is recommended that the LGU exercise its revenue generating power by collecting an entrance fee and cottage rental in Tinugdan spring.  Moreover, it is also recommended that management obtain title of the private properties surrounding the spring so as not to hinder future enhancements on the spring.

We recommend that the management justify the reason for the unequal distribution of funds and explain the difference between the cost and the estimated amount of goods distributed to the recipients. Moreover, the management should adhere to the provisions of AO No. 4 Series of 2011 and should distribute fairly the assistance received from the DSWD in the LGU’s subsequent supplemental feeding program.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with this audit recommendation.

It is further recommended that management refrain from indicating names of political figures in signboards/tarpaulins for projects owned by the government of the Republic of the Philippines.

Lastly, the management have to be cautious in the disbursement of funds by adhering to circulars, memorandums or orders.

observations.managementAction: Management has complied with this audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the office of the municipal mayor submit a justification for the above claim to prevent such transaction from being disallowed in audit.  Moreover, all public officials must adhere to COA Circulars and Memorandums in the disbursement of public funds in order to eliminate doubts in the mind of the constituents on the validity of the transaction entered upon.

We recommend that the management through its municipal accountant make the appropriate adjustment to ensure the accuracy and correctness of the inventory and expense accounts.   Moreover, management has to conduct a physical count of its inventories regularly and prepare a report of physical count of inventories (RPCI) and submit the same to the commission for proper verification. Lastly, management must maintain an inventory control system to avoid any misappropriations from occurring.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with this audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:
A periodic physical count of the LGU’s supplies and property must be conducted to properly establish the existence and accuracy of such accounts. 

The LGU’s property custodian has to prepare a Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and a Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in order to properly establish the existence and accuracy of the inventory and the PPE accounts.

The Municipal Treasurer’s office shall likewise maintain stock cards and property cards for supplies and PPE and reconcile the same with the stock cards or other property records such as the acknowledgment receipt for equipment (ARE) maintained by the accounting department.
observations.managementAction: On-going
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management broaden its dissemination of the invitation to bid so that other willing suppliers would be made aware of the projects of the LGU.  Moreover, the management through its Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should welcome all capable bidders to be able to select the one which offers the most benefit to the LGU.
observations.reason: Posted but no participating bidders.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that, management urge the concerned employees to liquidate the cash advances granted to them to prevent such from being disallowed in audit. observations.managementAction: Management has complied with this audit recommendation

observations.managementAction: Management has complied with this audit recommendation

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management implement the projects, activities and programs crafted for local poverty reduction in order to fully achieve the goals of grassroots participatory budgeting which is to reduce if not alleviate poverty within the municipality.
observations.reason: Careful planning is hereby enjoined

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management through the accounting department:

Seek the services of an expert (appraiser) to revalue the entity’s Property, Plant and Equipment.

Review the remaining useful lives of the PPEs on hand as of December 31, 2016.

Adjust the residual value of its Property, Plant and Equipment in order to accurately compute the depreciation expense.

Secure the commission a copy of the revalued PPE and reviewed useful lives in order to properly verify the value of the PPE, Depreciation, Accumulated Depreciation and Net Income accounts.

We recommend that management through the accounting department:

Establish the useful lives of the Infrastructure Assets on hand as of December 31, 2016.

Compute the depreciation expense for such infrastructure assets.

Make the necessary adjustments on the depreciation expense, accumulated depreciation – infrastructure assets and equity accounts, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with this audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Management have to fully utilize its appropriation for gender and development.  Moreover, management have to take into consideration the provisions of RA 9710, COA Circular 2014-001 and other GAD related laws and issuances in crafting projects, activities and programs financed by GAD funds so that, the appropriation will only be utilized for the purpose for which it is intended.

Recommendations:
Management have to exhaust all possible means to prevent, prepare for and mitigate the risks and the adverse effect of calamities by undertaking all projects intended for LDRRM.  
The MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant have to look into and reconcile the balance of the trust liabilities – disaster risk reduction and management fund account.
Management have to adhere to the provisions of RA 10121 in crafting its annual LDRRM action plan to ensure that only projects/programs/activities related to such will be crafted and implemented.

We recommend that the management require the coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer towards the implementation of the perpetual inventory method in the recording of supplies and materials to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with this audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to record the financial assistance as “Due from”/Receivable account and be expended only upon its proper liquidation by the implementing agency.  More importantly, require the recipient LGUs to submit the necessary liquidations.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with this audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management desist from granting advance payment on 15% mobilization cost on contracts entered into unless such requests are duly supported by an irrevocable standby letter of credit from a commercial bank, bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand issued by a surety or an insurance company duly license by the Insurance Commission pursuant to Annex E of the Revised IIR of RA 9184. 

We also recommend that recoupment/repayment schedules and other terms of agreement should be clearly stipulated in the contract documents in order to safeguard and protect the interest of the government should contractors fail to fulfill its obligations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:
1.    Require the persons making requests to list items with complete and specific descriptions.
2.    Require the BAC to carefully review the items first and not proceed with the Invitation to Bid if the items are not specifically described.
3.    Stop the practice of preparing the supporting documents only during the processing of payments.  The documents should be prepared at the appropriate time to aid the BAC in the procurement process and not just to support the claims for payment.
4.    Instruct the BAC to properly conduct the bidding procedures to conform with the rules and procedures of RA 9184.
